title: Probability, Counting, Expected Value, and Risk · Quantity Clinic: Probability Rules
summary: Compare warranties, games, reliability claims, and scheduling risks with transparent assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg probability rules increment in a practical risk and expected-value brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use complement, addition, and multiplication rules with explicit event relationships.
Define outcomes and sample spaces before assigning probabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Probability Rules evidence
steps.instruction: An outcome pays $35 with probability 0.35 and $0 otherwise. Calculate its expected value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Weight each outcome by its probability: 35 × 0.35 + 0 × 0.65.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend the Probability Rules model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Probability Rules applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
